metadata.title: Guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products in the treatment of hypertension

rules.rule_text: Blood pressure reduction is accepted as a valid surrogate endpoint for assessing antihypertensive efficacy. The goal of treatment is to prevent morbidity and mortality.
rules.verbatim_quote: Reduction in BP has usually been accepted as a valid surrogate endpoint in order to assess whether this goal can be achieved by an antihypertensive agent.
rules.exceptions: Morbidity/mortality claims require large-scale long-term controlled trials

rules.rule_text: Until morbidity/mortality data are available, the SmPC must specifically state that effects on mortality and cardiovascular morbidity are unknown.
rules.verbatim_quote: Until the results are available, it should be specifically mentioned in the SmPC that the effects on mortality and cardiovascular morbidity are unknown.
rules.exceptions: Not required if large-scale outcome trial results are available

rules.rule_text: SBP is the preferred primary efficacy variable; DBP is a mandatory secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: SBP is the preferred efficacy variable whilst DBP is a mandatory secondary end point.

rules.rule_text: BP lowering effects must be documented as pre-/post-treatment reduction.
rules.verbatim_quote: BP lowering effects of anti-hypertensive therapy should be documented as the pre-/post-treatment reduction of BP.

rules.rule_text: Response criteria for antihypertensive therapy include normalization of BP (SBP <140 mmHg and DBP <90 mmHg) and/or reduction of SBP >=20 mmHg and/or DBP >=10 mmHg.
rules.verbatim_quote: response criteria for antihypertensive therapy include the percentage of patients with a normalisation of BP (reduction SBP <140 mmHg and DBP <90 mmHg) and/or reduction of SBP >=20 mmHg and/or DBP >=10 mmHg.

rules.rule_text: The main endpoint should be BP at trough (residual effect at end of dose interval). The peak effect is the maximum BP reduction at steady state.
rules.verbatim_quote: The main endpoint should be BP at trough which is defined as the residual effect at the end of the dose interval. The peak effect is the maximum BP reduction (at steady state) identified in each patient compared to baseline following repeated BP measurements across a dose interval.

rules.rule_text: Trough-to-peak ratio must be pre-specified with a minimum effect threshold (e.g., 50% reduction in pre-dose BP) for the recommended dose range.
rules.verbatim_quote: Assessment of trough-peak ratio has to take into account methodological issues and a minimum effect should be pre-specified (e.g. 50% reduction in pre-dose BP) for the recommended dose range.

rules.rule_text: Calibrated sphygmomanometer is the standard method for BP measurement in pivotal trials. Aneroid manometer is not recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: Measurements with a calibrated sphygmomanometer are the standard method to determine BP in the setting of pivotal trials. If not available, another device may be used which is calibrated carefully in proportion to a mercury sphygmomanometer. Use of aneroid manometer is not recommended.
rules.exceptions: Alternative calibrated devices acceptable if mercury sphygmomanometer not available

rules.rule_text: Two or more readings separated by 2 minutes should be averaged. If SBP readings differ by more than 5 mmHg, additional readings must be obtained until stabilization.
rules.verbatim_quote: Two or more readings separated by 2 minutes should be averaged. If the first two readings of SBP differ by more than 5 mmHg, additional readings should be obtained until stabilisation has occurred with difference between these two readings within this limit.

rules.rule_text: BP must be checked in both arms simultaneously at least once. If inter-arm difference exceeds 20 mmHg SBP or 10 mmHg DBP on 3 consecutive readings, the patient should be excluded.
rules.verbatim_quote: BP should be checked simultaneously in both arms, at least once. BP should be recorded in the arm with the higher pressure; if differences between arms greater than 20 mmHg for SBP and 10 mmHg for DBP are present on 3 consecutive readings, the patient should be excluded from participating the study

rules.rule_text: Supine or sitting posture must be adopted for at least 5 minutes before measurement. For standing BP, the subject must stand for at least 1 minute. No shift between positions should occur during the study.
rules.verbatim_quote: Supine or sitting posture should be adopted for at least 5 minutes before measurement, and when standing BP is measured, the subject should be standing for at least 1 minute before measurement.

rules.rule_text: BP measurements must be standardized: same time of day, same arm, same personnel, same apparatus, and standardized conditions.
rules.verbatim_quote: BP should be measured under standardised conditions, as nearly as possible at the same time each day, on the same arm, by the same personnel, with the same apparatus.

rules.rule_text: ABPM is strongly recommended for evaluation of new antihypertensive agents but is not accepted as the sole basis for efficacy in an approval process.
rules.verbatim_quote: ABPM is strongly recommended for the evaluation of new antihypertensive agents. ... Therefore the measurement of ABPM is not currently accepted as the sole basis for efficacy in an approval process.
rules.exceptions: Can be used as supportive evidence

rules.rule_text: ABPM recorders must fulfill international acknowledged validation procedures (e.g., AAM-IBHS).
rules.verbatim_quote: The recorders used must fulfil international acknowledged validation procedures (e.g. AAM-IBHS).

rules.rule_text: At least 8 ABPM measurements must be included between 18 and 24 hours after drug intake. Suggested frequency: 2 readings day-time, 1 reading hourly night-time.
rules.verbatim_quote: At least 8 measurements should be included between 18 and 24 hours after drug intake. ... It is suggested that in day-time 2 readings and during night-time 1 reading hourly may provide an appropriate database.
rules.exceptions: Other properly justified and validated approaches may be accepted

rules.rule_text: Home BP measurement and ABPM cannot serve as the sole basis for efficacy evaluation in clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: there are insufficient data to accept self (home) measurement of BP as the sole basis for the evaluation of efficacy in clinical studies.

rules.rule_text: Central blood pressure measurement cannot currently be accepted as a sole basis for evaluation of efficacy in clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: the data based only on these measurements can currently not be accepted as a sole basis for evaluation of efficacy in clinical studies.
rules.exceptions: May be used as supplemental supportive data

rules.rule_text: Cardiovascular morbidity evaluation must include sequelae of severe organ damage (MI, heart failure, stroke, renal insufficiency). Use of standard definitions from clinical guidelines is encouraged.
rules.verbatim_quote: The evaluation of cardiovascular morbidity should especially take into account sequelae of severe organ damage (e.g. myocardial infarction, heart failure, stroke, renal insufficiency).
rules.exceptions: Softer endpoints (TIA, silent MI, unstable angina) are discouraged unless clinically justified

rules.rule_text: Blinded, centralized adjudication of causes of death and morbidity is required for mortality studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Blinded, centralised, adjudication regarding causes of death and morbidity will be necessary.

rules.rule_text: Study population must reflect intended clinical practice. Both genders must be included in a balanced way. Patients with more severe hypertension must also be evaluated.
rules.verbatim_quote: it is of utmost importance that the study population reflects the intended clinical practice. Patients of both genders should be included in studies in a balanced way. Patients with more severe stages of hypertension also need to be evaluated in studies.

rules.rule_text: Secondary hypertension and isolated systolic hypertension must be studied separately if such indications are claimed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with disorders causing secondary hypertension (e.g. phaeochromocytoma, adrenal adenoma, renal artery stenosis) and isolated systolic hypertension should be studied separately, if such an indication is specifically claimed.
rules.exceptions: Only applies when specific indication is claimed

rules.rule_text: Patients on existing antihypertensive therapy must undergo a wash-out period before trial enrollment. The duration depends on the half-life of the agent used.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients currently receiving antihypertensive therapy who are to be included should be withdrawn from current existing treatment during a wash-out. The time needed for wash-out will depend on the half-life of the agent(s) used and time taken for the BP to return to pre-treatment levels.
rules.exceptions: Patients with markedly elevated BP may require continuous therapy; add-on design appropriate

rules.rule_text: Initial elevated readings must be confirmed on at least two subsequent visits over one to several weeks. A run-in period of at least 2 weeks (sometimes 4 weeks) is essential.
rules.verbatim_quote: Initial elevated readings should be confirmed on at least two subsequent visits during one to several weeks. A run-in period of at least 2, sometimes as long as 4 weeks is essential before commencing a clinical trial of a new antihypertensive agent.

rules.rule_text: PK studies must be performed in the elderly and in patients with varying degrees of renal dysfunction and/or hepatic dysfunction.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special pharmacokinetic (PK) studies should be performed in the elderly and, depending on the route of elimination, in patients with varying degrees of renal dysfunction and/or hepatic dysfunction.
rules.exceptions: Renal/hepatic studies depend on route of elimination

rules.rule_text: Dose-response studies must be randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blinded, using at least 3 dosages to establish the clinically useful dose range and optimal dose.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dose-response studies should be randomised, placebo-controlled and double-blinded using at least 3 dosages to establish the clinically useful dose-range as well as the optimal dose.

rules.rule_text: Pivotal dose selection must be justified by dose-finding study results. Each recommended dose must demonstrate robust efficacy vs. placebo, and the added contribution of each dose must be shown.
rules.verbatim_quote: The dose schedule selected for pivotal studies must be justified on the basis of the results of the dose-finding studies in the target population. The results of the dose-response studies of a new antihypertensive agent should provide robust evidence of its efficacy as compared to placebo for each recommended dose. It is also essential to demonstrate the added contribution of each dose chosen.

rules.rule_text: Confirmatory trials must include comparison with an acknowledged standard antihypertensive agent of the same and of other therapeutic classes. A combination study with at least one other standard agent is mandatory.
rules.verbatim_quote: Controlled trials with reference therapy should be performed aiming at demonstration of (at least) a similar benefit/risk of the drug under investigation in comparison to an acknowledged standard antihypertensive agent of the same and of other therapeutic classes. ... A combination study with at least one other standard antihypertensive agent is mandatory.

rules.rule_text: Main dose-response studies must last at least 3 months, preferably 6 months. Each dose must be maintained over at least 4 weeks. Controlled studies with reference agents should last up to 6 months.
rules.verbatim_quote: Drug therapy in the main dose-response studies should last at least 3, preferably 6 months in order to demonstrate efficacy in terms of the antihypertensive effect and each tested dose should be maintained over at least 4 weeks when more than one dose is used. Controlled studies with reference agents should last even longer up to 6 months

rules.rule_text: A reasonable number of elderly patients (>65, >75, and >85 years) must be included in confirmatory studies. Subjects 75 years and older must be sufficient to assess both efficacy and safety.
rules.verbatim_quote: A reasonable number of elderly patients (>65 years, >75 and > 85 years, respectively) should be included in the therapeutic confirmatory studies. The number of subjects 75 years and older included in (pivotal) trials should be sufficient to assess both efficacy and safety in this group.

rules.rule_text: All adverse events must be fully documented with separate analysis of adverse drug events/reactions, dropouts, deaths while on therapy, and clinical laboratory results.
rules.verbatim_quote: All adverse events occurring during the course of clinical trials should be fully documented with separate analysis of adverse drug events/reactions, dropouts, deaths while on therapy and clinical laboratory results.

rules.rule_text: An overall safety plan for detection of adverse events must be prospectively designed early in development, optimally by Phase II. It must incorporate safety pharmacology and non-clinical toxicology findings.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the clinical studies, an overall plan for the detection and evaluation of potential adverse events, including justification of the size and duration of the studies with respect to the possibility of detecting safety signals, should be prospectively designed early during the clinical development, optimally by the time of phase II studies.

rules.rule_text: Withdrawal phenomena, especially rebound hypertension, must be studied specifically.
rules.verbatim_quote: Withdrawal phenomena, especially rebound hypertension, should be studied specifically.

rules.rule_text: Heart rate, ECG, and Holter monitoring must be performed at frequent intervals throughout the study to assess cardiac rhythm effects.
rules.verbatim_quote: Depending on the particular pharmacodynamic properties of the drug, heart rate, ECG and Holter monitoring should be performed at frequent intervals throughout the study.
rules.exceptions: Depends on pharmacodynamic properties of the drug

rules.rule_text: Effects on glucose and lipid metabolism must be evaluated with special attention as concomitant risk factors.
rules.verbatim_quote: As concomitant risk factors are often present at the same time, effects on glucose and lipid metabolism should be evaluated with special attention.

rules.rule_text: The MAA must adequately characterize the cardiovascular safety profile. This applies especially to products with a new mechanism of action or drug classes where CV safety is not established.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that the drug development programme, containing all relevant clinical and non-clinical data, adequately characterizes the cardiovascular safety profile enabling an evaluation of the cardiovascular safety in the marketing authorisation application (MAA). This refers in particular to products with a new mechanism of action or products belonging to a drug class for which the cardiovascular safety profile is not yet established or questioned

rules.rule_text: For FDCs, each active component must independently contribute to the positive evaluation of the combination. This is mandatory for marketing authorization.
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to obtain a marketing authorisation for a FDC, it is mandatory to prove that each active component in the scheduled dosage independently contributes towards the positive evaluation of the combination drug.

rules.rule_text: FDC clinical studies must state whether the FDC is for: 1) first-line therapy, 2) second/third-line therapy in non-responders, or 3) substitution therapy.
rules.source_section: 9.2 The clinical development of a fixed combination
rules.verbatim_quote: the wording of the indication must state clearly whether the FDC should be given as 1) first line therapy in patients receiving previously neither of the substances 2) second- or third-line therapy in non-responders to the mono-components, and 3) substitution therapy

rules.rule_text: For first-line FDC with subtherapeutic doses, three requirements must be met: documented contribution of each substance, at least similar efficacy to lowest approved monotherapy doses, and indication of reduced dose-dependent ADRs.
rules.verbatim_quote: The following minimum requirements have to be met if first-line therapy is claimed for a fixed low-dose combination. 1) Demonstration that each substance has a documented contribution within the (fixed) combination ... 2) Demonstration of at least similar efficacy to the lowest approved doses of each monotherapy compound ... 3) Indication for a reduction of (dose-dependent) adverse drug reactions by the low dose fixed combination as compared to the components in the lowest approved dosages

rules.rule_text: For first-line FDC with therapeutic doses, patients must have at least moderate or severe hypertension and/or high CVS risk. Pivotal evidence should come from treatment-naive patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: only patients with at least moderate or severe hypertension and/or at high risk for CVS disease are regarded to fit into the category with a high risk for inadequate BP control on mono-therapy. ... it is highly recommended that the pivotal body of evidence comes from studies conducted in treatment-naive patients

rules.rule_text: For second-line FDC add-on therapy, at least one or two pivotal clinical studies must demonstrate statistically significant and clinically relevant additional BP reduction in patients not controlled on monotherapy.
rules.verbatim_quote: at least one or two pivotal clinical study/-ies should be performed in a population of patients whose blood pressure cannot be normalised with one or all of the mono-components. A statistically significant and clinically relevant additional BP reduction of the combination should be demonstrated in patients who did not respond adequately to standard therapeutic doses of one or more of the mono-components.

rules.rule_text: For substitution FDC where all substances are well known, bioequivalence of the FDC to free combination is the pivotal requirement. Comparative PK data must demonstrate no interaction between components.
rules.verbatim_quote: In this case comparative PK data are needed, demonstrating that the two components of the FDC do not affect each other's PK patterns. Showing bioequivalence of the components in free combination with the FDC is the pivotal aspect in this setting.
rules.exceptions: Only when all substances are well known and joint application proven safe and efficacious

rules.rule_text: For second-line FDC indication, specific add-on trial requirements apply: indication for X-alone non-responders requires add-on trial to X; for Y-alone requires add-on trial to Y; for X-or-Y requires two add-on studies.
rules.source_section: 10. Addendum - 3. Clinical trials requirements for second line indication
rules.verbatim_quote: 3.1 In order to support the indication ... in patients whose blood pressure is not adequately controlled on X alone, at least one add-on trial to active treatment in non-responders to X should be carried out. ... 3.3 ... two add-on studies one in nonresponders to X and one with non-responders to Y should be carried out.
rules.exceptions: Data from parallel group comparative studies may support broader indication if only one add-on study conducted
